mnemonics: My Old Superb Pizzeria Favors Groups (MOSPF): Multicast Open Shortest Path First is used to send data to groups efficiently.
keyPoints: MOSPF is an extension to the OSPF unicast routing protocol to support multicast routing.
It uses the existing OSPF link-state database to build source-based multicast distribution trees for each group.
Routers use a special Link State Advertisement (LSA) to report group membership to all other routers in the area.
commonMistakes: Confusing MOSPF with DVMRP (Distance Vector Multicast Routing Protocol); MOSPF is link-state based, while DVMRP is distance-vector based.
Assuming MOSPF is efficient for wide-area networks; it is designed for and works best within a single OSPF area due to the flooding of group membership information.
